# Convert data frame of counts to dataframe of cases. The data frame must contain a column with frequencies (counts) as generated by as.data.frame from a contingency table

Convert data frame of counts to dataframe of cases. The data frame must
contain a column with frequencies (counts) as generated by as.data.frame
from a contingency table

## Usage

``` r
counts_to_cases(x, countcol = "Freq")
```

## Arguments

- x:

  a `data.frame` of counts generated from a contingency table.

- countcol:

  character string, name of the column of x containing the counts.
  Default name of the column is 'Freq'.

## Value

data frame of cases of dimension (total number of counts as sum of
'Freq' in x) times 2.

## Examples

``` r

counts_to_cases(as.data.frame(HairEyeColor[, , 1]), countcol = "Freq")
#>        Hair   Eye
#> 1     Black Brown
#> 1.1   Black Brown
#> 1.2   Black Brown
#> 1.3   Black Brown
```
